title: Classic German Braised Roast
description: A hearty and flavorful dish that's perfect for a chilly evening. This classic German braised roast is made with tender roast beef and a rich, savory sauce.

ingredients.name: beef roast (3 lbs)
ingredients.notes: Choose a chuck or round roast for the best flavor

ingredients.name: sour sauerbraten
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, use the liquid from the jar

ingredients.name: roast beef broth
ingredients.notes: Low-sodium broth for a richer flavor

ingredients.name: brown sugar
ingredients.notes: Balances out the tanginess of the sauerbraten

ingredients.name: black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground for the best flavor

ingredients.name: onion, sliced
ingredients.notes: Use a sweet onion like Vidalia for added sweetness

ingredients.name: caraway seeds
ingredients.notes: Traditional German spice for a distinct flavor

instructions: Season the roast with salt, pepper, and caraway seeds.
Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the roast until browned on all sides, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Remove the roast from the pot and set it aside.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until caramelized, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the sauerbraten, brown sugar, and beef broth to the pot. Stir to combine.
Return the roast to the pot and bring the liquid to a simmer.
Cover the pot with a lid and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Braise the roast for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until tender and falling apart.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Slice the roast against the grain and serve with the sauce spooned over the top.
